title: Async return types: Task, Task<T>, void

cards.prompt: Task has no value (done), Task<int> carries the value via await (42)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; async Task NoValue(){ await Task.Delay(1); Console.Write("done "); } async Task<int> WithValue(){ await Task.Delay(1); return 42; } await NoValue(); int r = await WithValue(); Console.WriteLine(r);

cards.prompt: async Task stores its exception in the task; await rethrows it so try/catch catches it
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; async Task Safe(){ await Task.Delay(1); throw new InvalidOperationException("boom"); } try { await Safe(); } catch (InvalidOperationException e) { Console.WriteLine($"Task caught: {e.Message}"); }

cards.prompt: ValueTask<int> is a value type (True); Task<int> is a reference type allocated from the heap (False)
